report_name: Whistleblower Lawsuit: Former Exec Accuses IBM of Covering Up Chinese State-Sponsored Hacking

threat_actors.name: Chinese state-linked hacking group
threat_actors.description: A hacking group associated with the Chinese government alleged to have perpetrated over 56,000 network breaches against IBM between 2013 and 2016 in a large-scale espionage campaign.
threat_actors.goals: Large-scale espionage, potentially for intellectual property theft and strategic information gathering.

identities.name: IBM
identities.description: A major U.S. technology provider alleged to have been persistently compromised by a Chinese state-linked hacking group and to have concealed the breaches from authorities and clients.

identities.name: William Barlow
identities.description: Former Vice President of Threat Intelligence at IBM who filed a whistleblower lawsuit alleging the company covered up thousands of data breaches.

identities.name: U.S. government
identities.description: The national government that was allegedly not notified of the breaches at IBM, depriving it of critical threat intelligence.
